stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML topics like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The source, Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicates its relevance within the open-source AI community.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The model is categorized under key ML tags like 'transformers' and 'llama', signaling its relevance in the open-source AI community.

stories.title: Jeff Bezos’ AI startup aims to build an ‘artificial general engineer’
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Jeff Bezos has launched a new AI startup named Prometheus.
The startup's goal is to develop an 'artificial general engineer' using AI.
Prometheus will focus on creating AI-powered tools to assist in the design of physical products.

stories.title: Anthropic shuts down Fable, Mythos models following Trump admin directive
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has ceased operations for its Fable and Mythos AI models.
The shutdown was reportedly due to a directive from the Trump administration's Commerce Department.
Concerns cited by the Commerce Department involved the potential for a Fable 5 'jailbreak' to pose a national security risk.

stories.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical 0-day vulnerability was discovered in PeopleSoft software, which is owned by Oracle.
The exploit allows attackers to steal significant amounts of data from affected organizations.
The severity of the vulnerability is described as being among the most critical in the industry.

stories.title: Ukraine's one-time test used fully autonomous drones to kill Russian soldiers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Ukraine demonstrated the use of fully autonomous drones in combat against Russian soldiers.
The report highlights the increasing integration of AI modules into Ukrainian drones and robotics.
The event signals a significant shift toward autonomous systems in modern warfare technology.

stories.title: Google sues Chinese cybercrime network that used Gemini to automate scams
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is taking legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network.
The criminals allegedly leveraged Gemini (Google's AI) to automate large-scale scams.
The scams reportedly targeted hundreds of thousands of individuals.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The research introduces 'EurekAgent,' an LLM-based agent designed to automate scientific discovery.

The core argument is that the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent's execution environment (resources, constraints, interfaces).
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions that can outperform human-designed methods.

stories.title: CRISPR tech selectively shreds cancer cells, including "undruggable" cancers
stories.author: gmays
bullet_points: CRISPR technology has shown potential in selectively destroying cancer cells.
The treatment approach is noted for its ability to target 'undruggable' cancers.
The news originates from Hacker News, suggesting high technical interest.

stories.title: Twenty One Zero-Days in FFmpeg
stories.author: redbell
bullet_points: Discovery of twenty-one zero-day vulnerabilities in FFmpeg.
FFmpeg is a critical, widely used multimedia framework across many applications.
This represents a significant security risk requiring immediate attention from developers and users.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes common ML frameworks and formats such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model responses.
The updated engine also delivers faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, aiming to provide the fastest local LLM experience on those devices.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and adoption of Apple's ML ecosystem.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (e.g., Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Agents-K1 introduces a new pipeline for scientific knowledge orchestration, addressing limitations in current LLM agents.
Existing LLM agents often simplify scientific papers to abstracts and citation graphs, missing crucial details like entities, claims, and evidence.
The system converts raw documents into a structured, 'agent-native' scientific knowledge format to improve reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method leverages operad theory, suggesting that an LLM's direct answer must match the answer derived from a stated decomposition of the same query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by diagnosing failures during inference without requiring ground-truth labels.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'EurekAgent,' an LLM-based agent designed for autonomous scientific discovery.
The research suggests the bottleneck in AI-driven science is shifting from defining agent workflows to engineering the execution environment.
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions that surpass human-designed methods.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) demonstrate that recursion over model calls is effective for long-context reasoning.
Production coding agents are increasingly using subagent spawning, exemplified by Anthropic's dynamic workflows.
The paper introduces the 'Recursive Agent Harness,' defining a pattern where the recursive unit is a full agent with tools (filesystem, code execution, planning), not just a model call.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language models (LLMs).
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the deep learning ecosystem.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for improved functionality on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: SpaceX’s massive IPO: all the latest news
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: SpaceX is conducting an Initial Public Offering (IPO) allowing public share purchases.
The IPO is for the combined entity encompassing rocket, AI, and social media ventures.
The financial event has reportedly increased Elon Musk's net worth to a trillionaire status.

stories.title: SpaceX is now a public company valued for its AI potential, so what comes next?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX has transitioned to a structure where investors demand profitability.
The focus of the company's valuation is increasingly tied to its AI potential.
The article prompts discussion on the future strategic direction of the now-investor-backed SpaceX.

stories.title: Controversial FISA spying law expires tonight. The spying will continue.
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) is set to expire.
Despite the expiration, the surveillance authority is extended via a certification until March 2027.
The story addresses a significant, ongoing debate regarding government surveillance powers.

stories.title: $130 billion in data center projects blocked by protests so far this year
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Significant financial impact: $130 billion in data center projects have faced roadblocks this year.
The conflict highlights growing public resistance to large-scale AI data center development.
The narrative suggests that successful local opposition provides a sense of 'political power' to activists.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning.
It proposes Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT) as a new post-training framework.
RA-RFT aims to improve language model reasoning by moving beyond simple lexical or semantic similarity matching.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous robotics, specifically articulated tool manipulation.
Introduces 'Mana' (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework for this task.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of coordinating internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions.

stories.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
stories.author: Min-Hung Chen
bullet_points: The research addresses the persistent challenge of spatial reasoning in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
It investigates how the design of the 'action interface' limits the effectiveness of tool-augmented agents.
The work specifically studies the impact of this interface design on an agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning.

stories.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
stories.author: Stefan Feuerriegel
bullet_points: The research proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the assessment of study reproducibility in the social and behavioral sciences.
This approach aims to overcome the resource-intensive and difficult-to-scale nature of traditional, manual reproducibility checks.
The study validates the LLM's capability by comparing its automated analysis against original findings across 76 published studies.

stories.title: Before You Think: System 0, AI-Mediated Cognition and Cognitive Colonization
stories.author: Giuseppe Riva
bullet_points: The paper analyzes three frameworks (Tri-System Theory, Thinkframes, System 0) regarding AI's cognitive impact.
It posits that 'System 0' offers a unique theoretical perspective on AI's influence that other models miss.
A core concept introduced is 'cognitive colonization,' describing how AI systems exert influence on cognition.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly increasing coverage depth for Slovak.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models on embedding tasks.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper recontextualizes 'Shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It proposes that automata-theoretic machinery (like specification compilation and product game construction) provides structural insights into adversarial networks.
The core argument is that focusing solely on runtime constraints misses the deeper analytical potential of these methods for system defensibility.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, a comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for Slovak.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly deepening coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: The Stable Recovery Manifold: Geometric Principles Governing Recoverability in Continual Learning
stories.author: Bhavika Melwani
bullet_points: The research investigates the geometric principles governing knowledge recoverability in continual learning, moving beyond the concept of catastrophic forgetting.
A new metric, Recovery Subspace Dimensionality (k_t), is introduced to quantify the complexity of recovering previously learned knowledge.
The study validates its findings using a sequentially trained ResNet-18 on the Split CIFAR-100 dataset.

stories.title: Aerial Wildfire Suppression Planning with a Hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata Fire Model
stories.author: Anthony Wong
bullet_points: Presents a novel framework for aerial wildfire suppression planning.
Combines a hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata model to predict fire spread based on terrain, fuel, and wind.
Integrates a gradient-based optimization module to design targeted aerial intervention strategies.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper re-frames 'Shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It argues that the underlying automata-theoretic machinery provides structural insights into adversarial networks rather than just enforcing constraints.
Key techniques mentioned include specification compilation, product game construction, and attractor computation.

stories.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
stories.author: Zhiyuan Hu
bullet_points: Introduces EvoArena, a new benchmark suite designed to test LLM agents in dynamic environments.
Addresses a key limitation in current LLM evaluations, which often assume static operational environments.
Focuses on the necessity for agents to continuously adapt their knowledge and behavior to changing conditions.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in complex reasoning tasks.
It argues that simple lexical or semantic similarity is insufficient for capturing underlying reasoning patterns.
A new framework, Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), is proposed as a post-training solution.

stories.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
stories.author: Pascal Denis
bullet_points: The research introduces 'Influcoder,' a new method for Data Attribution (DA) in Large Language Models (LLMs).
DA methods aim to quantify how specific training data samples influence a model's behavior (e.g., causing toxic outputs).
Influcoder distills gradient influence rankings from decoders into an encoder to achieve this attribution.

stories.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the 'execution-granularity mismatch' in tool-augmented LLM agents.
Critiques the common practice of exposing every atomic tool call in the reasoning trace.
Introduces 'HyperTool,' a unified, executable MCP-style tool interface to improve agent efficiency.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method leverages operad theory—a formalism for iterated substitution—to check if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived from a decomposed version of the same query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency and semantic entropy) for evaluating LLM reliability during inference.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, providing deep evaluation coverage for a low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models on embedding tasks.

stories.title: Statement on US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
stories.author: Dylan1312
bullet_points: The news concerns a statement from ClaudeDevs regarding a US government directive.
The directive specifically targets the suspension of access to models named Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
This indicates potential regulatory or geopolitical impact on AI model availability.
